title: Reading and Use of English - Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and six words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

questions.firstSentence: They think the manuscript was written in the 14th century.
questions.explanation: Tests complex passive with perfect infinitive.

questions.firstSentence: ‘I'll definitely drive you to the airport,’ said my uncle.
I didn't know the road was closed, so I was late for work.
questions.beforeGap: I wouldn't have been late for work
questions.explanation: Tests third conditional for hypothetical past.

questions.firstSentence: I'm sure he didn't see the 'No Parking' sign.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for negative past deduction.

questions.firstSentence: As soon as the meeting started, the fire alarm went off.
questions.afterGap: than the fire alarm went off.

questions.firstSentence: It's not worth trying to fix that old computer.
questions.afterGap: trying to fix that old computer.
questions.explanation: Tests the expression 'There is no point (in) + -ing'.
